dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4100 IS: To amend title 5, United States Code, to clarify whistleblower protections for duty speech disclosures, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Grassley (for himself and Mr. Wyden) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 5, United States Code, to clarify whistleblower protections for duty speech disclosures, and for other purposes.

section.header: Clarification of protections for duty speech whistleblowers
section.text: Section 2302(f) of title 5, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking (1) A and inserting A;
by redesignating subparagraphs (A) through (G) as paragraphs (1) through (7), respectively, and adjusting the margins accordingly;
in paragraph (6), as so redesignated, by striking or at the end;
in paragraph (7), as so redesignated, by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by inserting at the end the following:
the disclosure is made during the normal course of duties of an employee, the principal job function of whom is to regularly investigate and disclose wrongdoing.
